text.title: OpenAI Assistants API — Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure from input brief to signed-off output code.

list.item: Input: Instructions, tool list, model id.
Action: Run a one-time `assistants.create()` and pin the id in config.
Output: config.assistant_id.
Decision gate: If the id already exists in config, skip and re-use.
Input: Session storage (DB / cache).
Action: On session start `threads.create()`; on session end `threads.delete()`. Persist thread_id with session_id.
Output: DB row with (session_id, thread_id, opened_at, closed_at).
Decision gate: If session storage is ephemeral, set a TTL sweeper.
Input: File payloads.
Action: Upload via Files API; create named vector store; attach to assistant.
Output: vector_store with project-prefixed name.
Decision gate: If vector store > 1GB or > 100 files, split by topic.
Input: User message text.
Action: Append message → create run → poll OR stream → return assistant reply.
Output: Run completed in bounded time, response surfaced to user.
Decision gate: If run.status=`requires_action`, handle tool call; if `failed`, retry with backoff.
Input: Session end signal.
Action: Delete thread, decrement vector store reference, record cost row.
Output: Telemetry row written; resources released.
Decision gate: If cleanup fails, queue for nightly sweeper.
